common.recurringVirtualHint: Editing this occurrence creates a separate item from the series.

world.missingPathMessage:
Cannot find world path: {path}
Please select a new location.
world.duplicateRootDir: A world already uses this folder: {path}

network.name: Network Name
network.deleteSubtreeMessage: Exclude {count} nodes including the selected subtree from this network. The source object is not deleted.
network.deleteObjectMessage: Delete {name} from the world permanently. It will also disappear from every network where it is placed.
network.hierarchyLinkChildToParent: Hierarchy link: start from the child, then click the parent.
network.hierarchyDropToParent: Drop on the highlighted node to make it the parent.
network.hierarchyDropRootChild: Drop on empty hierarchy space to attach as a root child.
network.hierarchyDropKeepParent: Drop to keep the current parent and only move position.

ui.sectionDescription: Pick a meaning area first, then enable meanings and review the fields they create.
ui.propertiesFlowDescription: Enable meanings here, then immediately review and rename the generated fields below.
ui.semanticFieldsDescription: These fields are shaped by the selected meanings.
ui.customFieldsDescription: Attach at least one meaning to each field before choosing its type.
ui.noOptionalSlots: This meaning does not define optional slots.
ui.slotBindingHint: The app recognizes this field by meaning binding, not by the label you type.
ui.noMeaningsInCategory: This category does not have any meanings yet.
ui.meaningRequired: Select at least one meaning before choosing a type.

time.label: Time
time.description: Use for dates, durations, recurrence, and other time-based interpretation.

workflow.label: Workflow
workflow.description: Use for execution state, assignment, priority, progress, and operational tracking.

structure.label: Structure
structure.description: Use for parent-child structure, explicit ordering, tags, and taxonomy.

knowledge.label: Knowledge
knowledge.description: Use for sources, attachments, versions, and document-oriented context.

space.label: Space
space.description: Use for places, addresses, and coordinates that map the object into space.

quant.label: Quant
quant.description: Use for measurable values, targets, budgets, and numeric comparison.

governance.label: Governance
governance.description: Use for ownership, approvals, and system-level responsibility.

temporal.label: Temporal
temporal.description: Marks the schema as something that starts in time and may end later.

dueable.label: Dueable
dueable.description: Marks the schema as something that has a single due date or deadline.

recurring.label: Recurring
recurring.description: Adds recurrence rules so the object can repeat on a pattern.

statusful.label: Statusful
statusful.description: Adds a workflow state that board and list views can interpret.

assignable.label: Assignable
assignable.description: Adds assignee references so the object can be owned or worked by people.

prioritizable.label: Prioritizable
prioritizable.description: Adds a priority value for triage and ranking.

progressable.label: Progressable
progressable.description: Adds progress and completion state for tracking execution.

estimable.label: Estimable
estimable.description: Adds estimated and actual values for effort or resource comparison.

hierarchical.label: Hierarchical
hierarchical.description: Adds parent-child structure so objects can form a tree.

ordered.label: Ordered
ordered.description: Adds explicit ordering so objects can be sorted manually.

taggable.label: Taggable
taggable.description: Adds multi-tag classification for lightweight grouping.

categorizable.label: Categorizable
categorizable.description: Adds a single category key for taxonomy-style grouping.

sourceable.label: Sourceable
sourceable.description: Adds source metadata so the object can cite where it came from.

attachable.label: Attachable
attachable.description: Adds attachment references so files or assets can be linked.

versioned.label: Versioned
versioned.description: Adds version and supersession metadata for evolving documents or assets.

locatable.label: Locatable
locatable.description: Adds place and coordinate metadata so the object can be mapped.

measurable.label: Measurable
measurable.description: Adds a measurable value with unit and optional target.

budgeted.label: Budgeted
budgeted.description: Adds budget values and currency for financial planning.

approvable.label: Approvable
approvable.description: Adds approval state and approval metadata.

contains.label: Contains
contains.description: Represents containment, composition, or membership between two nodes.

entry_portal.label: Entry Portal
entry_portal.description: Marks the node that should open as the entry point for a contained structure.

parent.label: Parent Relation
parent.description: Represents a hierarchy parent-child relation between nodes.

references.label: References
references.description: Connects one object to another object it refers to.

explains.label: Explains
explains.description: Connects an object to another object it clarifies or explains.

evidence_for.label: Evidence For
evidence_for.description: Connects supporting evidence to a claim, conclusion, or object.

supports.label: Supports
supports.description: Connects an object to another object it positively supports.

contradicts.label: Contradicts
contradicts.description: Connects an object to another object it challenges or conflicts with.

causes.label: Causes
causes.description: Connects a cause to an effect or consequence.

depends_on.label: Depends On
depends_on.description: Connects an object to another object it depends on before it can proceed.

derived_from.label: Derived From
derived_from.description: Connects an object to the source object it was derived from.

summarizes.label: Summarizes
summarizes.description: Connects a summary to the object or content it condenses.

details.label: Details
details.description: Connects an object to another object that provides additional detail.

example_of.label: Example Of
example_of.description: Connects a concrete example to the object or concept it illustrates.

start_at.label: Start At
start_at.description: The start date or date-time the object begins from.

end_at.label: End At
end_at.description: The end date or date-time the object finishes at.

all_day.label: All Day
all_day.description: Whether the temporal object should be treated as an all-day item.

timezone.label: Timezone
timezone.description: The timezone used to interpret temporal values.

due_at.label: Due At
due_at.description: The due date or deadline for the object.

recurrence_frequency.label: Recurrence Frequency
recurrence_frequency.description: The base cadence, such as daily, weekly, or monthly.

recurrence_interval.label: Recurrence Interval
recurrence_interval.description: How many cadence units pass between repeats.

recurrence_weekdays.label: Recurrence Weekdays
recurrence_weekdays.description: The weekday set used by weekly recurrence.

recurrence_monthday.label: Recurrence Month Day
recurrence_monthday.description: The day of month used by monthly recurrence.

recurrence_until.label: Recurrence Until
recurrence_until.description: The date until which recurrence continues.

recurrence_count.label: Recurrence Count
recurrence_count.description: The number of repeat occurrences to generate.

status.label: Status
status.description: The current workflow state of the object.

status_changed_at.label: Status Changed At
status_changed_at.description: When the current status was last applied.

assignee_refs.label: Assignees
assignee_refs.description: The set of people or entities currently assigned.

primary_assignee_ref.label: Primary Assignee
primary_assignee_ref.description: The main assignee or owner of the active work.

priority.label: Priority
priority.description: A value used to rank urgency or importance.

progress_ratio.label: Progress Ratio
progress_ratio.description: A normalized progress value, usually between 0 and 1.

estimate_value.label: Estimate Value
estimate_value.description: The estimated amount of work or resource required.

estimate_unit.label: Estimate Unit
estimate_unit.description: The unit used for the estimate value.

actual_value.label: Actual Value
actual_value.description: The actual amount of work or resource consumed.

parent_ref.label: Parent
parent_ref.description: A reference to the parent object in a hierarchy.

order_index.label: Order Index
order_index.description: The explicit sort position used in ordered views.

tag_keys.label: Tags
tag_keys.description: A set of tags used for lightweight classification.

category_key.label: Category
category_key.description: A single category value used for taxonomy grouping.

source_url.label: Source URL
source_url.description: A URL pointing to the original source.

source_ref.label: Source Ref
source_ref.description: A reference to another object that acts as the source.

citation.label: Citation
citation.description: Citation text or source note associated with the object.

attachment_refs.label: Attachments
attachment_refs.description: References to attached files or external assets.

revision.label: Revision
revision.description: A revision marker or sub-version value.

supersedes_ref.label: Supersedes
supersedes_ref.description: A reference to the object this version replaces.

place_ref.label: Place
place_ref.description: A reference to the place associated with the object.

address.label: Address
address.description: A human-readable address or location string.

measure_unit.label: Measure Unit
measure_unit.description: The unit used for the measure value.

target_value.label: Target Value
target_value.description: The target value the measure is aiming for.

budget_currency.label: Budget Currency
budget_currency.description: The currency used for the budget amount.

budget_limit.label: Budget Limit
budget_limit.description: The maximum limit allowed for the budget.

owner_ref.label: Owner
owner_ref.description: The accountable owner of the object.

approved_by_ref.label: Approved By
approved_by_ref.description: The approver who accepted the object.

instance.visualHint: Set this instance's node visual here as the single source of truth.
instance.noFiles: No files linked to this instance
instance.noNetworkPlacement: No network node placement was found for this instance.

recurrence.description: Recurrence is a meaning made of cadence, interval, calendar constraints, and end conditions, not a single field.

schema.title: Schema
schema.name: Name
schema.fieldMeaningPrimitiveDesc: A value stored directly on this field, such as text, number, date, or static choices.
schema.fieldMeaningEmbedDesc: Expand another schema's properties inside the current object.
schema.fieldMeaningRefOneDesc: Choose one instance from another schema and link it here.
schema.fieldMeaningRefManyDesc: Choose multiple instances from another schema and link them here.
schema.propertySchemaFlowDesc: Start by deciding whether the field is a basic value, an included type, or a linked object. Then choose the detailed value type or target type.

fieldBehaviorDesc.none: Use the selected field type as-is.
fieldBehaviorDesc.schemaComposition: Edit another schema's fields inside this field.
fieldBehaviorDesc.schemaExtension: Start from an existing schema and add more fields.
fieldBehaviorDesc.conditionalField: Show this field only when a condition is met.
fieldBehaviorDesc.computedField: Calculate this value from other fields.
fieldBehaviorDesc.derivedCollection: Show a generated list of related objects.

schema.detachedSlotsWarningBody: These slots still keep their meaning bindings. Reassign or remove them if the meaning is no longer needed.
schema.requiredDesc: If left empty, this field still needs a value before saving.
schema.deleteConfirm: Delete this schema? Connected instances will not be deleted.
schema.detachMeaningConfirmMessage: Removing {meaning} detaches the meaning but keeps generated slots and stored values.

editor.modeSide: Set This Pane to Side Mode

settings.title: Settings
settings.sidebarTopItemsDesc: Reorder the primary sidebar views that stay above bookmarks.
settings.sidebarBookmarkedNetworksDesc: Pin world networks into the activity bar. Bookmarks appear between the upper and lower built-in groups.
settings.sidebarAvailableNetworksDesc: Add networks from the current world to the bookmark strip.
settings.sidebarBottomItemsDesc: Reorder the utility items that stay below bookmarks.
settings.sidebarNoWorld: Open a world to manage network bookmarks.
settings.sidebarNoAvailableNetworks: All current world networks are already bookmarked.
settings.appearanceModeDesc: Choose whether this window follows the system mode or always uses one brightness mode.
settings.modeDesc: Choose the brightness mode for the app.
settings.themeDesc: Choose the color theme for the app.
settings.networkViewerPlacementDesc: Choose which side the network view and side editor use in the main workspace. The default keeps the network on the left and the editor on the right.
settings.typographyDesc: Split interface, body, code, and terminal typography by role. Only fonts installed on this machine are listed in the selector.
settings.interfaceFontDesc: Menus, sidebars, dialogs, and general application chrome.
settings.interfaceFontSample: World graph, settings panels, and navigation labels.
settings.bodyFontDesc: Long-form writing surfaces such as notes, markdown, and multiline text fields.
settings.bodyFontSample: A calmer reading voice for instances, notes, and longer descriptions.
settings.codeFontDesc: Code editors, inline code, object ids, and monospace UI snippets.
settings.installedFontsHint: The selector only shows fonts currently installed on this machine. A safe fallback stack is still appended automatically.
settings.fontListUnavailable: Installed fonts could not be loaded right now.
settings.terminalPresetDesc: Switch fonts, spacing, and the base palette as a preset. Start with a small set of curated combinations instead of a free-form plugin system.
settings.terminalAppearanceDesc: Apply a preset, then tune the font, size, spacing, and padding directly. The background still follows the app's editor surface.
settings.terminalFontFallbackHint: A final system monospace fallback is always appended automatically.
settings.terminalCursorBlinkDesc: Choose whether the block cursor should blink.
settings.terminalWebglRendererDesc: Use the WebGL renderer when available. Turn it off if rendering becomes unstable.

hyper.label: Hyper Default
hyper.description: A dark preset tuned to stay close to Hyper's default feel.

netior.label: Netior Default
netior.description: The default font and spacing mix that stays closest to Netior's editor surface.

codex.label: Cascade Blue
codex.description: A cooler preset built around a Cascadia-first font stack and tighter spacing.

claude.label: Warm Contrast
claude.description: A warmer preset with an Iosevka-first stack and slightly looser line spacing.

powershell.label: Classic Console
powershell.description: A denser console preset with a slightly larger default size.

settings.savedPresetDesc: Saved separately and applied when this mode becomes active.
settings.currentThemeSetupDesc: This is the current light and dark assignment. Start here, then drill into a root theme below.
settings.themeLibraryDesc: Choose a root theme first, then decide whether to use it for light, dark, or both.
settings.editScopeDesc: Pick which mode slot this expanded theme editor should control.
settings.selectedFamilyPending: This family is not assigned yet
settings.selectedFamilyPendingBoth: Apply this root theme to both light and dark before editing variants and primary colors together.
settings.selectedFamilyPendingSingle: Apply this root theme to the selected mode before editing its variant and primary color.
settings.bothModesWillSync: Light and dark currently differ. Editing in Both mode will sync both slots to the same variant and primary setup.
settings.languageDesc: Choose the display language for the app.
settings.nativeAgentNotificationsDesc: When the window is minimized, show OS-level agent notifications instead of only keeping them inside the app.
settings.agentNotificationSoundsDesc: Play short in-app sounds for agent completion, attention requests, and errors.
settings.detachedAgentToastsDesc: Choose when detached windows should also show agent-complete toast notifications. Main window toasts always remain enabled.
settings.narreProviderDesc: Choose which provider Narre should use for new chats.
settings.narreApiKeyDesc: The key is stored in app config and Narre restarts automatically after saving.
settings.narreOpenAIKeyRequired: OpenAI provider needs an API key before Narre can start.
settings.narreOpenAIModelDesc: Optional override for the OpenAI model name. Leave blank to use the default.
settings.narreCodexAuthDesc: This provider uses your local Codex login instead of an API key.
settings.narreCodexAuthHint: Run `codex login` in a terminal, then choose Sign in with ChatGPT or API key.
settings.narreCodexAuthSetup: Narre will use the Codex CLI account already logged in on this machine.
settings.narreBehaviorDesc: Control how strongly Narre stays centered on Netior graph work instead of local coding-style exploration.
settings.narreDiscourageLocalWorkspaceActionsDesc: Keep Narre focused on graph objects and avoid unrelated file or repo analysis unless explicitly asked.
settings.narreExtraInstructionsDesc: Optional extra prompt guidance applied to Narre across providers.
settings.narreExtraInstructionsPlaceholder: Example: Prefer creating relation types before bulk instance creation.
settings.narreCodexRuntimeDesc: These options are passed only to Narre's Codex app-server launch and do not rewrite your global Codex CLI config.
settings.narreCodexModelDesc: Optional Codex model override for Narre.
settings.narreCodexUseWorldRoot: Use world directory as working directory
settings.narreCodexUseWorldRootDesc: When enabled, Narre points Codex at the target world directory instead of the Netior repo.
settings.narreRestartHint: Saving applies the provider settings and restarts Narre automatically.
settings.fieldComplexityDesc: Choose which field types the schema field builder shows by default. You can still reveal every type directly inside the picker when needed.
settings.fieldComplexityBasicDesc: Show only easy-to-understand field types like text, numbers, dates, and basic choices by default.
settings.fieldComplexityStandardDesc: Show the practical mix of reference and rich field types. Recommended for most users.
settings.fieldComplexityAdvancedDesc: Always show the more technical field types. Best when you actively design schemas.

narre.title: Narre
narre.homeSubtitle: Continue a conversation or start work with an agent team.
narre.chooseAgentHint: A 1:1 conversation starts from the selected agent's role and runtime settings.

network-builder.name: Network Builder
network-builder.description: Builds and expands Netior instance networks.

network-finder.name: Network Finder
network-finder.description: Finds relevant networks, instances, and paths.

agent-operator.name: Agent Operator
agent-operator.description: Coordinates agent sessions and orchestration flow.

agentTeamUi.agentListHint: Current session agents, assigned tasks, and status.
agentTeamUi.teamComposerHint: Mentions are available when the team session allows them.

tasks.operator: Keep the team conversation coherent and decide who should speak or act next.
tasks.ux: Review the group-chat flow and clarify what belongs in the session view.
tasks.code: Prepare the component changes after the interaction pattern is confirmed.

uxRedesign.title: Narre UX redesign
uxRedesign.subtitle: The operator explains the direction without showing a task graph.
uxRedesign.note1: The agent list lives only in the toggle sidebar.
uxRedesign.note2: Plans are written as operator messages.
uxRedesign.note3: Raw tasks, assignments, and events are hidden by default.

m1.speaker: You
m1.body: Rework Agent Team as a group chat. Do not show plans, task boards, or dependency graphs in the default view.

m2.speaker: Operator
m2.body: Understood. I will keep the main surface conversational. Internal tasks stay hidden, and agents will report progress as messages.

m3.speaker: UX Planner
m3.body: The first problem is orientation. A user should see a team conversation, not an orchestration dashboard.

m4.speaker: System
m4.body: Code Agent is waiting for the UI direction.

approval.title: Approval needed
approval.subtitle: A confirmation card appears inline and pauses the conversation.
approval.note1: Approval is a chat item, not a modal-first flow.
approval.note2: The sidebar shows which agent is blocked.
approval.note3: The next agent message waits until the approval is resolved.

m1.speaker: You
m1.body: Create a temporary Agent Team mock page so I can review several UX scenarios.

m2.speaker: Operator
m2.body: I will keep the real Agent Team entry intact and put the mock page behind a separate lab path.

m3.speaker: Code Agent
m3.body: The lab path needs its own navigation back to Narre Home and a toggle sidebar for the agent list.

m4.speaker: Approval
m4.body: Review this directly in the Agent Team screen?

completed.title: Completed work
completed.subtitle: Agents speak normally, then the operator posts a final summary.
completed.note1: Completion is a final operator message.
completed.note3: Debug details are not part of the default view.

m1.speaker: You
m1.body: Make the Agent Team experiment reviewable with mock data.

m2.speaker: Code Agent
m2.body: I added a group-chat mock surface with scenario switching and a toggle sidebar for the agent list.

m3.speaker: Test Agent
m3.body: The modified files parse correctly. Full typecheck is blocked by unrelated syntax errors in the current working tree.

m4.speaker: Operator
m4.body: Done. The default view stays conversational, agent list details live in the sidebar, and no task graph is shown.

set_field_behavior_dsl.description: Configure conditional, computed, or derived field behavior with validated Netior DSL generated by Narre from the user's domain request.

set_conditional_field_visibility.name: Set Conditional Field Visibility
set_conditional_field_visibility.description: Configure a field to appear only when another field, optionally through a referenced object field, equals a specific value.

validate_dsl.description: Validate a Netior DSL JSON AST expression or field behavior config.

set_interactive_view_preference.description: Set how a specific instance resolves its Interactive View: inherit the schema default, override with a template, or disable the view.

meaning.title: Meanings
